criteria.name: Completeness
criteria.description: Are all cognitive foundation components present (perception, memory, Gestalt, encoding, frameworks)?

scores.1: Major cognitive principles missing - significant gaps in foundation coverage
scores.2: Several important principles missing or incomplete - limited utility
scores.3: Core principles present but some supporting elements missing - adequate coverage
scores.4: Nearly complete with only minor gaps - good comprehensive coverage
scores.5: Exceptionally complete - all cognitive principles, frameworks, and domain guidance present

criteria.name: Clarity
criteria.description: Are cognitive principles explained clearly with WHY grounding?

scores.1: Principles confusing or presented as arbitrary rules without rationale
scores.2: Some clarity but WHY explanations insufficient - requires interpretation
scores.3: Generally clear with occasional ambiguity - mostly understandable
scores.4: Clear with good WHY explanations - easy to understand and apply
scores.5: Crystal clear with excellent WHY grounding and research citations

criteria.name: Actionability
criteria.description: Can principles be applied directly to design decisions?

scores.1: Theoretical only - no actionable guidance for designers
scores.2: Limited actionability - some guidance but difficult to apply
scores.3: Adequately actionable - can be applied with some effort
scores.4: Highly actionable - clear principles with practical application guidance
scores.5: Exceptionally actionable - principles immediately applicable with domain-specific examples

criteria.name: Structure
criteria.description: Is the skill logically organized with clear navigation between foundations, frameworks, and domains?

scores.1: Poorly organized - difficult to navigate between cognitive topics
scores.2: Weak organization - some structure but hard to find relevant principles
scores.3: Adequately organized - logical structure, acceptable navigation
scores.4: Well organized - clear path from foundations to frameworks to domains
scores.5: Exceptionally organized - intuitive navigation, linked resources, clear cross-references

criteria.name: Examples
criteria.description: Are sufficient concrete examples provided to illustrate cognitive principles?

scores.2: Minimal examples - insufficient to illustrate principles clearly
scores.3: Adequate examples - covers main principles with at least one example each
scores.4: Good examples - multiple concrete illustrations across domains
scores.5: Excellent examples - comprehensive illustrations for all major principles and domains

criteria.name: Triggers
criteria.description: Is when to use this skill vs related skills clearly defined?

scores.1: No trigger conditions - unclear when to use this vs evaluation/fallacy skills
scores.2: Vague triggers - difficult to know when this skill applies vs others
scores.3: Adequate triggers - general guidance on scope and boundaries
scores.4: Clear triggers - specific conditions with cross-references to related skills
scores.5: Excellent triggers - comprehensive when/when-not with explicit skill routing guidance

criteria.name: Cognitive Foundation
criteria.description: Are cognitive psychology principles well-explained with research backing?

scores.1: No cognitive foundation - prescriptive rules without research grounding
scores.3: Adequate foundation - basic principles with some research references
scores.4: Strong foundation - principles well-explained with author citations
scores.5: Exceptional foundation - deep principles, research citations, clear WHY for every recommendation

passing_note: Average score must be >= 3.5 for skill to be considered production-ready. Scores below 3 in any individual criterion require revision before deployment.
